rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF PAUL D. BRIARD
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/6/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (2) Did the administrator submit a Report of Operation of Law to the DOJ within 30 days of taking possession of the firearms? [Penal Code Sections 16990 and 27920.] Were the firearms sold through a licensed firearms dealer? [Penal Code Sections 27545 and 28050.] (3) Please file the bids obtained from other contractors to support the administrator's decision to hire her own son to prepare the condo for sale. (4) Paragraph 8 of the petition does not address whether or not the administrator gave notice of proposed action to dispose of the tangible personal property. [Prob. C. Sect. 10513.] (5) There are now four additional cousins (John Barrows, Larry Barrows, Lori Barrows Hoag, and Susan Barrows Lewis) who were not identified at the outset of these proceedings. No petition was filed pursuant to Probate Code Section 11700. [LRSF 14.35 H.] (6) These four additional purported heirs did not get (or waive) the initial Notice of Petition to Administer Estate. (7) When did Susan Barrows Lewis die? [LRSF 14.18 D.] (8) An accounting in a decedent's estate must begin with the date of death. [LRSF 14.34.] (9) No reconciliation has been shown for the difference between the ending balance in the WFB account xx84757 shown in the accounting and the balance shown on the bank statement. [LRSF 14.34 D.] (10) Each of the bank accounts has a balance that exceeds the FDIC insured amount. (11) Refunds that are for funds disbursed from the estate are not receipts includable in the fee base. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 9/22/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CRYSTAL LYNN HICKS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although the petition did not set forth assets on hand as required by CRC 7.651(b). Since the administrator is the sole heir, by virtue of his uncle's assignment of all the uncle's interest in the estate to the administrator, it is assumed that the administrator will file the appropriate receipt when seeking formal discharge. [LRSF 14.35 I.6.d.] NB: Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CANDIDA ROSA UMANZOR
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 09/08/2025. The following issues must be cleared or addressed two weeks before the date: (1) Need clarification of the decedent's share in the real property at the time of her death. (2) Need a copy of the deed that removed Oscar and Maria from the title, or, if applicable, a copy of the most recent title document following the 2012 deed. Simply combining the 1999 and 2012 deeds does not confirm that Thomas and Candida own 100% of the property despite the allegation made on the petition. If this petition pertains to the 50% ownership share of the entire real property, this information must be clarified by a verified supplement, accompanied by a copy of the most recent deed, unless the 2012 grant deed is the latest document. (3) Attachment 7a and 7b to identify the respective percentage or fractional interest subject to each relief. (4) Further explanation regarding the implications of the 2012 deed: It appears that "Thomas" granted his interest to both himself and the decedent. This action may have severed the joint tenancy, potentially altering each spouse's interest. Additionally, this raises questions about the validity of the transfer of community property by one spouse without the written consent of the other. (5) The proposed order must be submitted, clearing the above issues. Also, affirmation of #8 is not in line with the requests sought by the petition. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF STANLEY R. SCHMIDT
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/13/25. Examiner's Notes emailed to petitioner on 7/14/25 with the following issues: 1) The alternate executor, Kim Eagles-Smith, was not listed in Item 8. 2) Item 8 does not specify who the trustee of the trust referenced in Decedent's will is, who must be given notice. If Petitioner is the trustee, the beneficiaries of the trust must be listed / identified and given notice. 3) When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestors through whom they take (or would have taken) must be named and their relationships to the decedent and dates of deaths need to be stated [Local Rule 14.18C]. Please revise Item 8 of Petition. 4) A proposed nonresident personal representative will be required to post a bond to protect creditors, even if the will waives, or all heirs waive, bond. Please provide a declaration setting forth in detail the anticipated liabilities of the decedent and claims against the estate to be used by the Court to determine the amount of the bond. In no event will the bond be less than $20,000 [Local Rule 14.16F]. 5) Item 6f was marked but nephews and nieces (issue of deceased parents) are listed in Item 8. The first applicable box in 6 must be marked. 6) Proof of Publication has not been filed. 7) Proposed order has not been filed.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ANN MARCELLA FONTANELLA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/13/2025. The following issues to be cleared before 8/6/2025. 1. A verified supplement is needed to provide the following information: (1) 1. Identify the acting trustee of the sole devisee trust. (2) 2. Clarify whether there are any other issues of the decedent's grandparents, who are not beneficiaries under the terms of the trust, as Petition #8 lists multiple cousins as trust beneficiaries. (3) Identify the deceased ancestor through whom the relatives-heirs would have inherited if the decedent had died intestate, along with the ancestor's relationship to the decedent. This information should have been included in Attachment 8, and those persons should be noticed. This information is for the court to ascertain that all heirs at law are properly noticed. (4) Petition #8 to include the alternative executor, Mietta Rigali. 2. Notice Petition to Administer Estate and Notice of Directions for Remote appearance and Access must be given to Mietta Rigali. Prob. Code sec. 8110(b) and LRSF 14.1.C. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.
